items.e: Negative agreement: Neither + have + I. No 'not' needed because 'neither' is already negative.
items.ed: Negative Übereinstimmung: Neither + have + I. Kein 'not' nötig, da 'neither' bereits negativ ist.

items.p: "She is very tired." — Which is the correct agreement?
items.h: The original uses 'is' (be). What form of 'be' goes with 'I'?
items.hd: Der Originalsatz verwendet 'is' (be). Welche Form von 'be' passt zu 'I'?
items.e: The original uses 'is' (= be). With 'I', we use 'am': So am I.
items.ed: Der Originalsatz verwendet 'is' (= be). Mit 'I' verwenden wir 'am': So am I.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: "I like football." — "So I do."
items.h: Check the word order after 'So'. Is the auxiliary in the right place?
items.hd: Überprüfe die Wortstellung nach 'So'. Ist das Hilfsverb an der richtigen Stelle?
items.e: The correct order is So + auxiliary + subject: So do I, not 'So I do'.
items.ed: Die richtige Reihenfolge ist So + Hilfsverb + Subjekt: So do I, nicht 'So I do'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: "I can't ride a bike." — "So can't I."
items.h: The original is negative. Should you use 'So' or 'Neither'?
items.hd: Der Originalsatz ist negativ. Solltest du 'So' oder 'Neither' verwenden?
items.e: For negative agreement, use 'Neither', not 'So'. And no 'not/n't' needed: Neither can I.
items.ed: Für negative Übereinstimmung verwende 'Neither', nicht 'So'. Und kein 'not/n't' nötig: Neither can I.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: "She has visited Paris." — "So do I."
items.h: The original uses 'has' (present perfect). Does 'do' match?
items.hd: Der Originalsatz verwendet 'has' (Present Perfect). Passt 'do' dazu?
items.e: The auxiliary must match: 'has visited' = present perfect, so use 'have' (with 'I'): So have I.
items.ed: Das Hilfsverb muss übereinstimmen: 'has visited' = Present Perfect, also 'have' (mit 'I'): So have I.

items.p: Your friend says: 'I enjoy reading.' You feel the same! Agree using 'So... I.'
items.h: 'Enjoy' is present simple. Use 'do' for present simple agreement.
items.hd: 'Enjoy' ist Present Simple. Verwende 'do' für Present-Simple-Zustimmung.
items.e: So + auxiliary + I. Present simple: So do I. = I enjoy reading too.
items.ed: So + Hilfsverb + I. Present Simple: So do I. = Ich lese auch gerne.

items.p: Your friend says: 'I won't be at the party.' You can't go either. Agree using 'Neither... I.'
items.h: Negative agreement: Neither + the SAME auxiliary + I. 'Won't' uses 'will'.
items.hd: Negative Zustimmung: Neither + dasselbe Hilfsverb + I. 'Won't' verwendet 'will'.
items.e: Neither + auxiliary + I for negative agreement. Won't → Neither will I.
items.ed: Neither + Hilfsverb + I für negative Zustimmung. Won't → Neither will I.

items.p: Your friend says: 'He is tired.' You're tired too! Agree using 'So... I.'
items.h: 'Is' is a form of 'be'. For 'I', use 'am'. So am I.
items.hd: 'Is' ist eine Form von 'be'. Für 'I' verwende 'am'. So am I.
items.e: So + matching auxiliary + I. 'Is' → 'am' for I: So am I. = I'm tired too.
items.ed: So + passendes Hilfsverb + I. 'Is' → 'am' für I: So am I. = Ich bin auch müde.

items.p: 🇩🇪 "Ich mag Musik." — "Ich auch."
a: I like music. — So do I.
I like music. So do I.
I like music — So do I
items.h: 'Ich auch' after a positive sentence = 'So + auxiliary + I'.
items.hd: 'Ich auch' nach einem positiven Satz = 'So + Hilfsverb + I'.
items.e: German 'Ich auch' (me too) after a positive sentence = 'So do I' (present simple).
items.ed: Deutsch 'Ich auch' nach einem positiven Satz = 'So do I' (Simple Present).

items.p: 🇩🇪 "Ich kann nicht kochen." — "Ich auch nicht."
a: I can't cook. — Neither can I.
I can't cook. Neither can I.
I cannot cook. Neither can I.
items.h: 'Ich auch nicht' = 'Neither + auxiliary + I'. Match the auxiliary to 'can'.
items.hd: 'Ich auch nicht' = 'Neither + Hilfsverb + I'. Passe das Hilfsverb an 'can' an.
items.e: German 'Ich auch nicht' (me neither) = 'Neither can I'. The auxiliary matches 'can' from the original.
items.ed: Deutsch 'Ich auch nicht' = 'Neither can I'. Das Hilfsverb stimmt mit 'can' aus dem Original überein.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: have / so / I
items.h: The pattern is: So + auxiliary + subject.
items.hd: Das Muster ist: So + Hilfsverb + Subjekt.
items.e: The correct word order is: So + auxiliary + subject: So have I.
items.ed: Die richtige Wortstellung ist: So + Hilfsverb + Subjekt: So have I.

items.p:
Match each statement with the correct agreement response:
1. "I love cats."
2. "I can't drive."
3. "I have visited Berlin."
4. "I am hungry."
5. "I don't like spiders."
a. So have I.
b. Neither do I.
c. So am I.
d. So do I.
e. Neither can I.
items.h: Match the auxiliary: present simple → do, can → can, present perfect → have, be → am/is/are.
items.hd: Passe das Hilfsverb an: Simple Present → do, can → can, Present Perfect → have, be → am/is/are.
items.e: 1-d: love (present simple) → So do I. 2-e: can't → Neither can I. 3-a: have visited → So have I. 4-c: am → So am I. 5-b: don't like → Neither do I.
items.ed: 1-d: love (Simple Present) → So do I. 2-e: can't → Neither can I. 3-a: have visited → So have I. 4-c: am → So am I. 5-b: don't like → Neither do I.

items.p: Your friend says: "I like ice cream." You agree. Write your response using 'So... I.'
items.h: To agree with a positive present simple statement, use 'So + do/does + subject'.
items.hd: Um einem positiven Simple-Present-Satz zuzustimmen, verwende 'So + do/does + Subjekt'.
items.e: 'I like' is present simple. To agree: So + do + I = So do I.
items.ed: 'I like' ist Simple Present. Um zuzustimmen: So + do + I = So do I.
